channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Customers seek refunds after AVG Travels cancels China tour packages at short notice
item.description: Hundreds of Australian travellers were left stranded and seeking refunds after Melbourne-based company AVG Travels cancelled multiple China holiday packages at the last minute. Customers reported rece...

item.title: Putin threatens retaliation after deadly strike on Luhansk student dormitory
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has vowed retaliation following a deadly attack on a student dormitory in occupied Luhansk, which Moscow claims killed six people and injured 39. Fifteen individuals r...

item.title: Overview of Proposed Social Media Regulation Bills in the Philippine Congress
item.description: In the 20th Congress of the Philippines (2025-2028), lawmakers are considering multiple bills to regulate social media platforms due to concerns about disinformation, child safety, and tech accountabi...

item.title: Child protection strategy in South Africa faces implementation and funding concerns
item.description: Child safety advocate Anzio Jacobs argues that South Africa’s newly approved National Strategy to Accelerate Action for Children (NSAAC) will fail to protect vulnerable children unless government comm...

item.title: How American Home Design Evolved in the Early 20th Century
item.description: The article explores the transformation of American residential architecture during the early 1900s, a period marked by significant shifts in both design philosophy and everyday living standards. At t...

item.title: Cool and Unsettled Weekend Ahead with Rain and Storms in Eastern Regions
item.description: This weekend, South Africa will experience cool and unsettled weather across much of the country. A cold front is moving through, bringing showers and thunderstorms mainly to the central and eastern p...

item.title: Kiraitu Murungi Opens Up About Grief After Death of Daughter Anita Kendi
item.description: Former Meru governor Kiraitu Murungi has emotionally spoken about the pain of losing his daughter, Anita Kendi, who passed away on May 14. Speaking during her burial ceremony, the veteran politician n...

item.title: Gladys Wanga Gives Gor Mahia KSh 2 Million Support Ahead of FKF Premier League Decider
item.description: Homa Bay governor and ODM chairperson Gladys Wanga hosted Gor Mahia players, officials and the technical bench at her Kochia residence ahead of the club’s crucial FKF Premier League clash against Mara...

item.title: PM Shehbaz begins official China visit focused on investment, CPEC cooperation and strategic ties
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif departed for China on a four-day official visit aimed at further strengthening Pakistan-China strategic cooperation and boosting economic engagement between the two count...

item.title: Palarong Pambansa 2026 Kicks Off in Agusan del Sur with Student-Athletes Competing Nationwide
item.description: For the first time in its history, the Palarong Pambansa has arrived in Agusan del Sur, hosting the 66th edition of the national grassroots multi-sports event from May 24 to 31. Student-athletes from ...

item.title: Fatboy Slim headlines BBC Radio 1's Big Weekend with a dance-filled set
item.description: Fatboy Slim, the legendary British DJ, closed the dance-focused opening day of BBC Radio 1's Big Weekend at Herrington Country Park in Sunderland with a lively, nostalgia-driven headline set. The 62-y...

item.title: Authorities Removed Firearms from Mosque Shooter’s Home Before 2026 Attack
item.description: In 2025, more than a year before a deadly attack at a San Diego mosque, local authorities had already intervened in one of the eventual shooter’s homes due to concerns about his behavior. Police filed...

item.title: Norman Seeff Captures Early Moments of Patti Smith and Robert Mapplethorpe in New York
item.description: In 1969, South African photographer Norman Seeff met Patti Smith and Robert Mapplethorpe, then young and relatively unknown, at Max's Kansas City, a popular New York club for artists and musicians. St...

item.title: Cannes Film Festival 2026: Anticipation Builds Ahead of Palme d'Or Ceremony
item.description: The 79th Cannes Film Festival is set to conclude with the presentation of the Palme d’Or, cinema’s most prestigious award. This year’s edition has been considered somewhat lacklustre, with Hollywood l...

item.title: Bob Weir of the Grateful Dead: A Look Back at His Early Years and Legacy
item.description: Bob Weir, the co-founder and rhythm guitarist of the Grateful Dead, passed away on January 10, 2026, at the age of 78. He died peacefully with family by his side after battling cancer and enduring und...

item.title: Bakwata Kilimanjaro to Distribute Meat from 500 Cattle to Needy During Eid Al-Adha
item.description: Bakwata Kilimanjaro, the Islamic Council of Tanzania, is preparing for one of the biggest charitable gestures this Eid Al-Adha by slaughtering 500 cattle. The meat will be distributed to wahitaji, mea...

item.title: US Secretary of State Rubio visits India to strengthen ties and attend Quad meeting
item.description: United States Secretary of State Marco Rubio has started his first ever visit to India, arriving just a week after the US held a friendly summit with China. His four-day tour covers four cities, begin...

item.title: Gov Otu Urges Party Unity and Commitment After APC Primaries in Cross River
item.description: For Cross River State, Governor Bassey Otu don call on APC members make dem maintain unity, reconciliation, and follow the party principles sharply after the just-finished primaries. During the unveil...

item.title: NBI Prepares to Serve Additional ICC Arrest Warrants Including Bato Dela Rosa
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) in the Philippines has confirmed that additional arrest warrants from the International Criminal Court (ICC) may soon be issued against several personalities...

item.title: U.S. Invests $2 Billion in Quantum Computing to Strengthen Technology and Prepare for Cryptography Challenges
item.description: The U.S. government is investing over $2 billion in quantum computing, signaling its strategic importance for both technology and national security. The Department of Commerce announced letters of int...
